maml:description.maml:para: Grants one or more users delegate access to a target mailbox.
Calls POST /api/user/add-delegate-user for each primary address supplied. Both addresses are sent in the request body -- no address in the URL path.
Request body: { "data": [{ "primaryAddress": "<mailbox-owner>", "delegateAddress": "<user-getting-access>" }] }
Default mode: primaryAddress = DelegateAddress (the mailbox being shared) delegateAddress = each PrimaryAddress (the user receiving access)

Inverted mode (-InvertedMode switch, for shared/functional mailboxes): primaryAddress = each PrimaryAddress delegateAddress = DelegateAddress Use if the default mode returns errors indicating an address is not found as a primary mailbox holder.
The mailbox to be shared (the mailbox others will access).
One or more email addresses that will receive delegate access. Accepts pipeline input and array values.
Mimecast API 2.0 gateway base URL. Default: https://api.services.mimecast.com
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client ID.
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client Secret as a SecureString.
Milliseconds to pause between API calls. Default: 300.

Use this switch when DelegateAddress is a shared/functional mailbox that is not a full user account in Mimecast. Inverts the request body so each PrimaryAddress becomes the body's primaryAddress field and DelegateAddress becomes the delegateAddress field. Use if the default mode returns errors indicating an address is not found as a primary mailbox holder.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The mailbox to be shared (the mailbox others will access).
One or more email addresses that will receive delegate access. Accepts pipeline input and array values.

Use this switch when DelegateAddress is a shared/functional mailbox that is not a full user account in Mimecast. Inverts the request body so each PrimaryAddress becomes the body's primaryAddress field and DelegateAddress becomes the delegateAddress field. Use if the default mode returns errors indicating an address is not found as a primary mailbox holder.
Mimecast API 2.0 gateway base URL. Default: https://api.services.mimecast.com
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client ID.
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client Secret as a SecureString.
Milliseconds to pause between API calls. Default: 300.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Standard mode - full user mailbox $secret = Read-Host 'Client Secret' -AsSecureString Add-MimecastDelegateUsers ` -DelegateAddress 'shared@contoso.com' ` -PrimaryAddresses 'alice@contoso.com','bob@contoso.com' ` -ClientId $env:MC_CLIENT_ID ` -ClientSecret $secret

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Inverted mode - shared/functional mailbox as target $secret = Read-Host 'Client Secret' -AsSecureString $users = Import-Csv .\delegates.csv | Select-Object -ExpandProperty PrimaryAddress Add-MimecastDelegateUsers ` -DelegateAddress 'materialreceipt@contoso.com' ` -PrimaryAddresses $users ` -InvertedMode ` -ClientId $env:MC_CLIENT_ID ` -ClientSecret $secret

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # WhatIf preview - no changes made Add-MimecastDelegateUsers -DelegateAddress 'shared@contoso.com' ` -PrimaryAddresses 'alice@contoso.com' @creds -WhatIf

maml:description.maml:para: Grants one or more users delegate access to a mailbox using Mimecast API 1.0.

Calls POST /api/delegate/add-delegate for each primary address supplied. Use this function when your Mimecast tenant is on Email Security Cloud Integrated or the US-B data center (login-usb.mimecast.com), where the API 2.0 User Management endpoints return 404.

Credentials are obtained from Mimecast Admin Console: Administration -> Services -> API 1.0 Applications (App ID + App Key) Account -> Roles -> [role] -> API Application Authentication (Access Key + Secret Key - per user/application binding)
The mailbox to be shared (the one others will gain access to).
One or more email addresses that will receive delegate access.

Mimecast API 1.0 base URL. Use the URL matching your login DC: US-A : https://us-api.mimecast.com US-B : https://usb-api.mimecast.com EU : https://eu-api.mimecast.com DE : https://de-api.mimecast.com CA : https://ca-api.mimecast.com
Application ID from the API 1.0 application registration.
Application Key from the API 1.0 application registration.
Access Key from the API application authentication binding.
Secret Key from the API application authentication binding (SecureString).
Milliseconds to pause between API calls. Default: 300.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The mailbox to be shared (the one others will gain access to).
One or more email addresses that will receive delegate access.

Mimecast API 1.0 base URL. Use the URL matching your login DC: US-A : https://us-api.mimecast.com US-B : https://usb-api.mimecast.com EU : https://eu-api.mimecast.com DE : https://de-api.mimecast.com CA : https://ca-api.mimecast.com
Application ID from the API 1.0 application registration.
Application Key from the API 1.0 application registration.
Access Key from the API application authentication binding.
Secret Key from the API application authentication binding (SecureString).
Milliseconds to pause between API calls. Default: 300.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Bulk add from CSV $secretKey = Read-Host 'Secret Key' -AsSecureString $v1Creds = @{ BaseUrl = 'https://usb-api.mimecast.com' AppId = 'your-app-id' AppKey = 'your-app-key' AccessKey = 'your-access-key' SecretKey = $secretKey } $users = Import-Csv .\delegates.csv | Select-Object -ExpandProperty PrimaryAddress Add-MimecastDelegateV1 -DelegateAddress 'shared@company.com' ` -PrimaryAddresses $users @v1Creds | Format-Table PrimaryAddress, Success, FailReason -AutoSize

maml:description.maml:para: Returns the list of users who have delegate access to a mailbox.
Calls POST /api/user/find-delegate-users with the primary address in the request body and returns structured delegate objects.

The API response shape is: { "data": [ { "delegateUsers": [...] } ], "fail": [] }. The delegateUsers array is nested inside data[0]. Each entry is mapped to a PSCustomObject with: EmailAddress, DisplayName, DelegateId, Source, PrimaryAddress.

SHARED MAILBOXES: Delegate relationships added with -InvertedMode are stored against each user's account in Mimecast, not the shared mailbox. Querying the shared mailbox address directly returns an empty list. Supply the user's address as -PrimaryAddress and filter results by EmailAddress to list or remove those relationships.
The email address of the mailbox whose delegates you want to list.
Mimecast API 2.0 gateway base URL. Default: https://api.services.mimecast.com
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client ID.
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client Secret as a SecureString.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The email address of the mailbox whose delegates you want to list.
Mimecast API 2.0 gateway base URL. Default: https://api.services.mimecast.com
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client ID.
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client Secret as a SecureString.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $secret = Read-Host 'Client Secret' -AsSecureString Get-MimecastDelegateUsers -PrimaryAddress 'shared@contoso.com' ` -ClientId $env:MC_CLIENT_ID -ClientSecret $secret | Format-Table EmailAddress, DisplayName, DelegateId, Source -AutoSize

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Export delegate list to CSV $secret = Read-Host 'Client Secret' -AsSecureString Get-MimecastDelegateUsers -PrimaryAddress 'shared@contoso.com' ` -ClientId $env:MC_CLIENT_ID -ClientSecret $secret | Export-Csv -Path "Delegates_$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss').csv" -NoTypeInformation

maml:description.maml:para: Returns users who have delegate access to a mailbox using Mimecast API 1.0.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
The shared mailbox whose delegate list you want to retrieve.
Mimecast API 1.0 base URL (default: https://usb-api.mimecast.com).
Application ID from the API 1.0 application registration.
Application Key from the API 1.0 application registration.
Access Key from the API application authentication binding.
Secret Key from the API application authentication binding (SecureString).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The shared mailbox whose delegate list you want to retrieve.
Mimecast API 1.0 base URL (default: https://usb-api.mimecast.com).
Application ID from the API 1.0 application registration.
Application Key from the API 1.0 application registration.
Access Key from the API application authentication binding.
Secret Key from the API application authentication binding (SecureString).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Revokes a delegate user's access to a mailbox.
Calls POST /api/user/remove-delegate-user with the primary address and delegate record ID in the request body. Use Get-MimecastDelegateUsers first to obtain the DelegateId.
The email address of the mailbox to remove the delegate from.
The delegate record ID returned by Add-MimecastDelegateUsers or Get-MimecastDelegateUsers.
Mimecast API 2.0 gateway base URL. Default: https://api.services.mimecast.com
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client ID.
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client Secret as a SecureString.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The email address of the mailbox to remove the delegate from.
The delegate record ID returned by Add-MimecastDelegateUsers or Get-MimecastDelegateUsers.
Mimecast API 2.0 gateway base URL. Default: https://api.services.mimecast.com
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client ID.
Your Mimecast API 2.0 Client Secret as a SecureString.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # WhatIf preview - no changes made Remove-MimecastDelegateUser -PrimaryAddress 'shared@contoso.com' ` -DelegateId 'abc-123' @creds -WhatIf

maml:description.maml:para: Revokes a user's delegate access to a mailbox using Mimecast API 1.0.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
The shared mailbox to remove the delegate from.
The email address of the user whose delegate access will be removed.
Mimecast API 1.0 base URL (default: https://usb-api.mimecast.com).
{{ Fill AppId Description }}
{{ Fill AppKey Description }}
{{ Fill AccessKey Description }}
{{ Fill SecretKey Description }}
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The shared mailbox to remove the delegate from.
The email address of the user whose delegate access will be removed.
Mimecast API 1.0 base URL (default: https://usb-api.mimecast.com).
{{ Fill AppId Description }}
{{ Fill AppKey Description }}
{{ Fill AccessKey Description }}
{{ Fill SecretKey Description }}
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Probes known API path variants to identify the correct delegate endpoint.

Tests several path prefix combinations (with and without /api/) using a known valid user address to determine which variant the tenant responds to. Run this once when debugging 404 errors to confirm the correct path.
{{ Fill KnownUserEmail Description }}
{{ Fill BaseUrl Description }}
{{ Fill ClientId Description }}
{{ Fill ClientSecret Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill KnownUserEmail Description }}
{{ Fill BaseUrl Description }}
{{ Fill ClientId Description }}
{{ Fill ClientSecret Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
